key_insight: The paper extends adaptive polynomial chaos expansion to multiple quantities of interest, using multivariate active learning to choose samples that improve all outputs jointly.
engineering_relevance: This helps engineers build cheaper and more reliable surrogate models for finite element structures when several responses must be predicted from the same input set.
method: Sequentially sample from a candidate pool using each point's contribution to output variance
Balance exploration of the input space with exploitation of aggregated variance across all outputs
Generalize adaptive PCE from scalar to vector-valued quantities of interest
evidence_signal: Across several engineering examples, the proposed sequential strategy outperformed Latin Hypercube Sampling in surrogate accuracy, stability, and second-order statistical estimates.
watch_item: Because sample selection depends on variance aggregation across outputs, performance may be sensitive to candidate-pool quality and to how strongly the different QoIs are correlated.
paper_title: Uncertainty Quantification of Engineering Structures by Polynomial Chaos Expansion and Multivariate Active Learning
